mission.title: argo-rollouts: Add an option to proceed to the next rollout step based on a percentage of available replicas
mission.description: Add an option to proceed to the next rollout step based on a percentage of available replicas. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-rollouts deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-rollouts version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
helm list -n argo-rollouts 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-rollouts installation.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
Add an option to allow Argo Rollouts to proceed to the next step in a rollout even if the desired replica count has not been fully achieved. Instead, allow progression based on a predefined percentage of the new ReplicaSet’s replicas being available.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add an option to proceed to the next rollout step based on…
steps.description:
## Context
As of the date of writing this PR, whenever you want to promote your canary for each and every step, Argo Rollouts waits for the number of available pods grow and equal the *exact* number of desired pods that the step calls for. For example...
1. Argo Rollouts sees that the canary is
```yaml
func allDesiredAreAvailable(rs *appsv1.ReplicaSet, desired int32) bool {
    return rs != nil && desired == *rs.Spec.Replicas && desired == rs.Status.AvailableReplicas
}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add an option to proceed to the next rollout step based on…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Context
As of the date of writing this PR, whenever you want to promote your canary for each and every step, Argo Rollouts waits for the number of available pods grow and equal the *exact* number of desired pods that the step calls for. For example...
1. Argo Rollouts sees that the canary is passing all metric checks, analysis runs, etc.
